issues.title: Utility file uses camelCase but exports a constant map
issues.description: The file `lucideIconMap.ts` is named in camelCase which is correct for a utility file. However, the file primarily exports a constant `ICON_MAP` and a utility function `getIcon`. The naming is consistent with conventions (SCREAMING_SNAKE for the exported constant, camelCase for the function). No issue here upon closer inspection.
issues.fix: No fix needed — naming is correct.

issues.title: Duplicate GalleryView interface defined in two files
issues.description: The `GalleryView` interface is defined in both `ImageGallery.tsx` (line 17) and `GalleryOverlays.tsx` (line 6) with identical shape `{ label: string; index: number }`. While not strictly a naming issue, this creates a maintenance concern — the type should be defined once and shared.
issues.fix: Extract `GalleryView` to a shared types file (e.g., `product-detail/types.ts`) and import from both components.

issues.title: Local type alias `TFn` uses abbreviation
issues.description: The type alias `TFn` is an abbreviation that reduces readability. Convention prefers descriptive PascalCase names for types. A clearer name would be `TranslationFn` or `TranslateFn`.

issues.title: Import order: same-directory imports before absolute imports
issues.description: In ProductCard.tsx, the same-directory imports (`./ProductCardImage`, `./ProductCardMeta`) appear at lines 8-9 before the feature/shared absolute imports at lines 11-14. Convention expects: React -> Third-party -> Shared -> Feature -> Local. Local (same-directory) imports should come last.
issues.fix: Move same-directory imports (`./ProductCardImage`, `./ProductCardMeta`) below the `@/features/...` and `@/shared/...` imports.

issues.title: Import order: same-directory import before absolute imports
issues.description: In ImageGallery.tsx, the same-directory import `./GalleryOverlays` (line 7) appears before absolute imports `@/features/...` (lines 9-10). Same-directory imports should come after absolute imports.
issues.fix: Move `import { GalleryOverlays } from './GalleryOverlays'` below the `@/features/...` imports.

issues.title: Import order: same-directory imports before absolute imports
issues.description: Same-directory imports (`./ImageGallery`, `./PriceBlock`, `./RatingStars`) appear at lines 8-10, before feature-level absolute imports at lines 12-14.
issues.fix: Move same-directory imports after the `@/features/...` imports.

issues.title: Import order: relative imports before absolute imports
issues.description: Relative imports from `../components/` (lines 8-11) appear before absolute imports `@/features/...` and `@/mocks/...` (lines 13-17). By convention, relative imports should come after absolute imports.
issues.fix: Move `../components/*` imports after the `@/features/...` and `@/mocks/...` imports.

issues.title: Component grouping folder uses kebab-case — correct
issues.description: The `product-detail/` folder groups related product detail components. Using kebab-case for this grouping folder is correct per naming conventions (feature folders use kebab-case). All individual component files within use PascalCase correctly.
issues.fix: No fix needed — naming is correct.

issues.title: Well-structured naming: Context + Provider + Hook pattern
issues.description: CartContext.tsx exports `CartProvider` and `useCart` following the naming convention perfectly: Provider suffix for the provider component, `use` prefix for the hook. Constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (`COOKIE_KEY`, `COOKIE_MAX_AGE_S`). Action types use SCREAMING_SNAKE string literals.

issues.title: Domain types follow PascalCase convention consistently
issues.description: All interfaces (`Product`, `ProductImage`, `PhysicalDetails`, `CommissionDetails`, `TicketDetails`, `DigitalDetails`, `ProductHighlight`, `ProductScreenshot`, `ProductFaq`, `ProductSeller`, `ProductReview`, `ProductSpec`) and type aliases (`ProductType`, `ProductCategory`) use PascalCase correctly. Props interfaces use the `Props` suffix where applicable.

issues.title: Exported constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE correctly
issues.description: `PRODUCT_TYPES`, `CATEGORY_THEMES`, `PRODUCT_CATEGORIES` all use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for exported global constants. The `CategoryTheme` interface uses PascalCase. The utility function `getCategoryTheme` uses camelCase with a verb prefix. All correct.
